title: AUC-First Rank Blend Across Binary, Survival, And Pairwise Models
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering quality, not probability calibration.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering quality, not probability calibration. CatBoost and LightGBM expose ranking objectives, while a survival model orders rows by remaining-stint risk differently from a plain binary classifier. A rank-space blend is a concrete extension of prior ensemble ideas and better aligned with the competition metric.
implementation_hint: Train three OOF models with race-aware folds: a strong binary GBDT, the censored remaining-stint model, and a local group ranker using CatBoost PairLogit/LambdaMart or LightGBM `lambdarank` on race-lap candidate groups. Convert each prediction stream to fold-wise ranks or normal scores, then average with CV-tuned weights.
expected_effect: Small but plausible gain from objective diversity: different models may sort borderline pit-window rows differently even when their calibrated probabilities look similar.
risk: If group definitions are weak, the ranker adds noise; weight fitting must stay strictly OOF and not drift into public-LB tuning.
